page: Vultr vx1-g-4c-16g is a general-purpose virtual server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor running at 2.0 GHz. The configuration includes 4 shared vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via 1 GB of network-attached storage, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU hardware is present.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong single-threaded capabilities. Single-core stress-ng CPU performance and single-threaded gzip compression both rank in the top tier, placing in the top 10% of tested instances. However, multi-threaded performance is more moderate due to the shared CPU allocation. Multi-core stress-ng CPU performance, PassMark CPU scores, and memory bandwidth tests fall within the average range. Notably, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is relatively weak, landing in the bottom 25% of tested servers.

Given its shared CPU model and balanced 1:4 CPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance offers a cost-effective option for workloads that require high single-core burst capacity but do not continuously saturate all virtual cores. It is well-suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, Redis key-value databases, and lightweight background processing tasks. It is less optimal for sustained multi-threaded compute-intensive tasks or large-scale language model inference.
description: Vultr vx1-g-4c-16g is a general-purpose virtual server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 4 shared vCPUs on an AMD EPYC-Turin x86_64 processor running at 2.0 GHz. Equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM and 1 GB of network storage, this server delivers a balanced resource profile. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU and single-threaded gzip compression tests scoring in the top 10%. Conversely, multi-threaded performance is average, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is weak, reflecting the shared nature of the CPU allocation. This server represents a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring strong single-thread execution without continuous multi-core saturation. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, Redis databases, and lightweight background processing.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-4c-16g is a general-purpose server with 4 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 1 GB network storage, offering top-tier single-core performance for web serving and databases.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-g-4c-16g is a general-purpose server featuring 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and top-tier single-core benchmark performance.
tagline: A balanced virtual server featuring AMD EPYC processing, strong single-core performance, and cost-efficient shared resources for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 4-core AMD EPYC-Turin x86_64 processor at 2.0 GHz
Equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM and 1 GB of network-attached storage
Top-tier single-core performance in stress-ng and gzip benchmarks
Shared CPU allocation suitable for bursty, non-continuous workloads
Optimal for static web serving, Redis databases, and general-purpose tasks
